About: It is about a market in which one person or company is the only supplier of a particular good or service.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with unfair price raises.
CausedBy: It is caused by mergers and integrations.
It is caused by forming naturally.
It is caused by being established by a government.
HasA: It has a lack of economic competition to produce a particular thing.
It has a lack of viable substitute goods.
It has the possibility of a high monopoly price well above the seller's marginal cost.
It has high barriers to entry.
It has a single seller of the good who produces all the output.
It has market power.
It has economies of scale.
It has capital requirements.
It has technological superiority.
It has control of natural resources.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a lack of economic competition to produce a particular thing.
It is characterized by a lack of viable substitute goods.
It is characterized by the possibility of a high monopoly price well above the seller's marginal cost.
It is characterized by a single seller.
It is characterized by high barriers to entry.
IsA: It is a market in which one person or company is the only supplier of a particular good or service.
It is a single seller.
It is a business entity that has significant market power.
It is a market situation in which there is only one buyer.
It is a form of oligopoly.
It is a situation in which one or a few entities have market power.
It is a structure in which a single supplier produces and sells a given product or service.
It is a pure monopoly.
It is a monopolistic competition market.
It is a perfect competition market.
KnownAs: It is known as a pure monopoly.
KnownFor: It is known for a lack of economic competition to produce a particular thing.
It is known for a lack of viable substitute goods.
It is known for the possibility of a high monopoly price well above the seller's marginal cost.
It is known for a single seller.
It is known for high barriers to entry.
PartOf: It is part of market structures.
It is part of traditional economic analysis.
UsedAs: It is used as an example of government-granted monopolies.
UsedFor: It is used for providing an incentive to invest in a risky venture.
It is used for enriching a domestic interest group.
It is used for management structures.
It is used for industrial organization.
It is used for economics of regulation.
UsedIn: It is used in a market.
UsedTo: It is used to gain the ability to raise prices or exclude competitors.
It is used to charge overly high prices.
It is used to distort the market.
